Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Team SA news: Chad le Clos shrugs off injury concerns

South Africa’s most decorated Olympian, Chad le Clos, has arrived at the athletes’ village at the Paris Olympics 2024.

South Africa’s most decorated Olympian,Chad le Clos, has arrived at the athletes’ village at the Paris Olympics 2024 ready to make the most of his fourth experience at the greatest show on earth.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section

Le Clos joins only five other athletes to have done that in the country’s history.

His four medals, one gold and three silver, are also the most of any South African Olympian, while he has a list of short-course achievements that could fill a library, making him one of the greatest swimmers the country has produced.

Now 32, he is taking to the water in the100m butterflyand admits that he has a lot to do.

“I’m the third oldest male swimmer at these Games, so in those terms I’m something of a pensioner. And in spite of picking up a shoulder tear in training, I’ve come to Paris believing I have a chance. I’ve raced with injuries before.”

Le Clos is a leader and a racer and although the odds seem to be against him adding to his medals tally in Paris, one also needs to acknowledge that if he gets to the final it’s game on.

“I think that the semi-final will be the harder race, because there will be 16 swimmers going for a place in the final. I’m hoping to get there and once I do I’ll be chasing another dream. I’m a warrior, a born racer.”

Chad le Clos appeared toplay down the severity of his injuryat a media conference.

“Yes, I’ve had an injury the last two weeks. I went to the medical team as soon as I arrived from Montpellier and had it checked out. The first thing I did after that was head for the dining hall!

“I’m here to enjoy the experience, even if I don’t think it’s my last, and give of my best.”
